extreme_observation: A sales representative had their worst month ever, selling only 2 units against a team average of 20.
intervention: Their manager gave them an intensive one-on-one coaching session and a revised territory.
followup_result: The rep sold 18 units the following month, close to the team average.

extreme_observation: A student scored in the bottom 5th percentile on a standardized test after a week of illness.
intervention: The school enrolled them in an expensive private tutoring program.
followup_result: The student scored at the 45th percentile on the next test.

extreme_observation: A basketball player had an exceptional game, scoring 52 points — nearly double their season average of 28.
intervention: The coach increased their playing time and adjusted offensive plays to feature them.
followup_result: The player scored 26 points in the next game, close to their season average.

extreme_observation: A manufacturing line produced a defect rate of 15% in one batch, far above the usual 2% rate.
intervention: Management implemented a new quality control protocol and additional worker training.
followup_result: The next batch had a 2.5% defect rate, nearly back to normal.

extreme_observation: A hedge fund had its best-ever quarter, returning 40% when the market returned 8%.
intervention: Investors poured in additional capital and analysts credited the fund manager's superior skill.
followup_result: The fund returned 9% the following quarter, in line with the market.
